ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of The Estate
Off Calendar Jan-29-2024 Continued To Apr-08-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Chelsea Blake appeared by zoom in propria persona.

The court noted that the probate examiner suggested that Ms. Blake submit her questions in advance of the hearing. Ms. Blake stated that she did not submit her questions because she filed her petition. The clerk confirmed that an accounting/petition for final distribution has been entered with a hearing date of April 8, 2024. Status on close of estate is also continued to that date. The court informed Ms. Blake that she needs to comply with the examiner's request and that if she has any questions, she is to contact Mr. Miyauchi.

Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Singh. Not reported.

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Oct-11-2023 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On The Filing Of The Inventory And Appraisal
Off Calendar Oct-11-2023 Off Calendar

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Oct-11-2023 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On The Filing Of The Inventory And Appraisal
Off Calendar Jul-31-2023 Continued To Oct-11-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

An Order to Appear and Order to Show Cause will be issued to the Administrator Chelsea Blake to appear on October 11, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to show why she should not face suspension, or sanctions in the amount of $250.00 if an inventory and appraisal, or a petition for termination of the estate for lack of assets is not filed by September 27, 2023. Status on filing of the inventory and appraisal is also continued to October 11, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Singh. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On The Filing Of The Inventory And Appraisal
Off Calendar May-24-2023 Continued To Jul-31-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Cecilia Blake (propria persona) appearing via zoom (administrator).

Mrs. Blake spoke of how complicated this has been, stated she is in the process of collecting her mother's final paycheck and working with the HR department, that she is trying to file the taxes, that she has no record any physical property and that she only found one bank account. Mrs. Blake also asked the court if it would be acceptable for her to file an incomplete inventory and appraisal, stated that the estate value is well below the $166,000.00 threshold, that the value is $50,000.00 to $80,000.00, and that she has not heard from any debt collectors. In addition, Mrs. Blake stated that she is not touching anything, that everything is just sitting there, that she was in California to open the estate account, that there is a lot of back and forth with Robinhood and Google, and that she is willing to pursue other avenues but is limited to the scope of things. Furthermore Mrs. Blake stated that the estate account has $42,000.00 in it, that her mother's final paycheck is less than $5,000.00 and that she has mother's W-2 form from 2021. The Court informed Mrs. Blake of the option of filing a partial inventory of appraisal and noted that the mother's prior tax return may show any interest she may have earned on any accounts. Status on filing of inventory and appraisal is continued to July 31, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross Moody. Clerk: Ateesh Datt. Deputy: Choi. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petition For Letters Of Administration
Off Calendar Jan-04-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom: Chelsea Blake appearing via Zoom (Pro Per Petitioner). Jordan Blake appearing via Zoom (Interested Party).

The Court states that it was waiving the defect in the publication as to the middle initial. Jordan Blake states that he is in support of the petition. The Court inquires as to Ms. Blake's background and her understanding as to her obligations if appointed. In response to the Court, Ms. Blake states that her mother died without a will, that she has no access to her mother's accounts, that she understands her obligations if appointed, that she is to close out her mother's affairs, that any medical or other bills need to be settled, and that everything needs to be in order before arrangements can be made for any division of property. Ms. Blake notes that her mother worked for a nonprofit, states that they are not aware of any real property, and that there should only be personal effects such as jewelry, etc. The Court addresses Ms. Blake and states that she needs to open and keep a separate account, that she is to pay any valid claims, and that no distributions can be made without a court order. Ms. Blake notes that she donated some of her mother's clothing. The Court states that it was referring to any money/funds.

At the conclusion of comments, the Court grants the petition for letters of administration. Chelsea Blake is appointed administrator with full authority to administer the estate under the Independent Administration of Estates Act. Bond is set at $20,000.00. (Order signed in open court.)

Status on the filing of the final inventory and appraisal is set for May 24, 2023, at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Status on close of the estate is set for January 29, 2024, at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Ms. Blake stated that she was approved for the bond and that there was one small issue that needed to be resolved as to her apartment number.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: K. Saephon/H. Singh. Matter not reported.
